methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $640M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Redwood Materials
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series D), post-money $6B+

data.narrative:
Solugen, a Series D Climate & Energy company that has raised $640M+, is evaluated against a universe of frontier-tech peers spanning 1 public company and 8 private firms at similar or adjacent stages. The peer set includes Redwood Materials ($6.0B, Series D), KoBold Metals ($2.0B, Series C), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B, Series C), and Amogy ($700M, Series C).

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis uses the $640M raised as a base and applies a 1.5x multiplier typical of Series D to next-stage advancement, yielding $960M. Peer-median analysis across the four same-sector comparables produces a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range spanning $700M to $6.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and capital intensity within climate tech.

Synthesizing both approaches produces a valuation range of $816M to $1.7B, with a median of $1.2B. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound, while peer median anchors the midpoint. Key uncertainties include the appropriate stage-step multiplier for Solugen's specific path to commercialization and whether peer comparables adequately reflect differences in technology risk, capital requirements, and time-to-revenue between hydrogen production (Solugen's focus) and competing climate solutions represented in the comp set.
